---
applyTo: "**/locales/**"
---

# Internationalization (i18n) Instructions

## CRITICAL RULE
**Every user-facing string MUST be translated.** Never use hardcoded English text in JSX.

## Supported Languages
- `en` — English (primary)
- `ta` — Tamil

## File Structure

```
apps/form-app/src/locales/
├── en/                      # English translations
│   ├── common.json
│   ├── dashboard.json
│   ├── formBuilder.json
│   ├── signIn.json
│   ├── ... (110+ namespace files)
├── ta/                      # Tamil translations
│   ├── (mirror en/ structure)
└── index.ts                 # Namespace registry
```

## Adding a New Translation Namespace

### 1. Create JSON Files

`apps/form-app/src/locales/en/myComponent.json`:
```json
{
  "titles": {
    "main": "My Component",
    "sub": "Subtitle"
  },
  "buttons": {
    "save": "Save",
    "cancel": "Cancel",
    "delete": "Delete"
  },
  "messages": {
    "success": "Operation completed successfully",
    "error": "Something went wrong"
  },
  "labels": {
    "name": "Name",
    "description": "Description"
  }
}
```

`apps/form-app/src/locales/ta/myComponent.json`:
```json
{
  "titles": {
    "main": "என் கூறு",
    "sub": "துணைத்தலைப்பு"
  },
  "buttons": {
    "save": "சேமி",
    "cancel": "ரத்து",
    "delete": "நீக்கு"
  }
}
```

### 2. Register Namespace

In `apps/form-app/src/locales/index.ts`, import and add the namespace:
```typescript
import myComponentEn from './en/myComponent.json';
import myComponentTa from './ta/myComponent.json';

// Add to the translations object
const resources = {
  en: {
    // ... existing
    myComponent: myComponentEn,
  },
  ta: {
    // ... existing
    myComponent: myComponentTa,
  },
};
```

### 3. Use in Components

```typescript
import { useTranslation } from '../hooks/useTranslation';

function MyComponent() {
  const { t } = useTranslation('myComponent');

  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{t('titles.main')}</h1>
      <p>{t('messages.success')}</p>
      <button>{t('buttons.save')}</button>
    </div>
  );
}
```

## Usage Patterns

```typescript
// ❌ WRONG — hardcoded text
<p>Loading field analytics...</p>
<button>Save Changes</button>

// ✅ CORRECT — translated
<p>{t('messages.loading')}</p>
<button>{t('buttons.save')}</button>

// ✅ With interpolation
<p>{t('messages.count', { values: { count: 10 } })}</p>
// JSON: "count": "{{count}} items found"

// ✅ Nested keys
{t('errors.validation.required')}
```

## JSON Naming Conventions

Follow this grouping pattern in translation files:
- `titles` — Page/section headings
- `buttons` — Button labels
- `labels` — Form labels, field names
- `messages` — Success/info messages
- `errors` — Error messages
- `tooltips` — Tooltip text
- `placeholders` — Input placeholders
- `loading` — Loading state text
- `empty` — Empty state text
- `confirmations` — Confirmation dialog text

## Existing Namespaces (110+ files)

Key namespaces include:
`common`, `dashboard`, `formBuilder`, `formBuilderHeader`, `signIn`, `signUp`, `responses`, `formAnalytics`, `fieldSettings`, `sharing`, `plugins`, `templates`, `pricing`, `formSettings`, `formDashboard`, and many more.

Always check if a relevant namespace already exists before creating a new one.
